paths.niti: Path of Ethics — for right conduct, moral dilemmas, integrity
paths.vyavahara: Path of Worldly Wisdom — for practical life, social skills, street smarts
paths.prerana: Path of Inspiration — for motivation, courage, perseverance
paths.viveka: Path of Discernment — for judgment, critical thinking, choosing wisely

slokas.sanskrit: विद्या नाम नरस्य रूपमधिकं प्रच्छन्नगुप्तं धनम्। विद्या भोगकरी यशःसुखकरी विद्या गुरूणां गुरुः। विद्या बन्धुजनो विदेशगमने विद्या परा देवता। विद्या राजसु पूज्यते न हि धनं विद्याविहीनः पशुः॥
slokas.transliteration: vidyā nāma narasya rūpam adhikaṁ pracchanna-guptaṁ dhanam | vidyā bhoga-karī yaśaḥ-sukha-karī vidyā gurūṇāṁ guruḥ | vidyā bandhujano videśa-gamane vidyā parā devatā | vidyā rājasu pūjyate na hi dhanaṁ vidyā-vihīnaḥ paśuḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Knowledge is a person's greatest beauty, a secretly guarded treasure. Knowledge brings enjoyment, fame, and happiness; knowledge is the teacher of teachers. Knowledge is a kinsman in foreign lands; knowledge is the supreme deity. Knowledge is honored by kings, not mere wealth — one without knowledge is an animal.
slokas.when_to_use: When you need motivation to keep learning, or when someone undervalues education over material wealth.

slokas.sanskrit: साहित्यसङ्गीतकलाविहीनः साक्षात्पशुः पुच्छविषाणहीनः। तृणं न खादन्नपि जीवमानस्तद्भागधेयं परमं पशूनाम्॥
slokas.transliteration: sāhitya-saṅgīta-kalā-vihīnaḥ sākṣāt paśuḥ puccha-viṣāṇa-hīnaḥ | tṛṇaṁ na khādann api jīvamānas tad-bhāgadheyaṁ paramaṁ paśūnām ||
slokas.english_meaning: One who is devoid of literature, music, and art is verily an animal without a tail and horns. That such a one lives without eating grass is indeed the greatest fortune of animals.
slokas.when_to_use: When reflecting on the importance of cultivating artistic and cultural sensibilities beyond mere material pursuits.

slokas.sanskrit: सत्सङ्गत्वे निस्सङ्गत्वं निस्सङ्गत्वे निर्मोहत्वम्। निर्मोहत्वे निश्चलतत्त्वं निश्चलतत्त्वे जीवन्मुक्तिः॥
slokas.transliteration: sat-saṅgatve nissaṅgatvaṁ nissaṅgatve nirmohatvam | nirmohatve niścala-tattvaṁ niścala-tattve jīvan-muktiḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Good company leads to non-attachment; non-attachment leads to freedom from delusion; freedom from delusion leads to steadfastness in truth; steadfastness in truth leads to liberation in this very life.
slokas.when_to_use: When choosing your circle of friends or when reflecting on how the company you keep shapes your character and destiny.

slokas.sanskrit: दुर्जनः परिहर्तव्यो विद्ययालङ्कृतोऽपि सन्। मणिना भूषितः सर्पः किमसौ न भयङ्करः॥
slokas.transliteration: durjanaḥ parihartavyo vidyayālaṅkṛto 'pi san | maṇinā bhūṣitaḥ sarpaḥ kim asau na bhayaṅkaraḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: A wicked person should be avoided even if adorned with learning. Is a serpent not fearsome even though it is adorned with a jewel on its hood?
slokas.when_to_use: When someone impressive on the surface turns out to have bad character, or when you need to distance yourself from toxic but talented people.

slokas.sanskrit: क्षणशः कणशश्चैव विद्यामर्थं च साधयेत्। क्षणे नष्टे कुतो विद्या कणे नष्टे कुतो धनम्॥
slokas.transliteration: kṣaṇaśaḥ kaṇaśaś caiva vidyām arthaṁ ca sādhayet | kṣaṇe naṣṭe kuto vidyā kaṇe naṣṭe kuto dhanam ||
slokas.english_meaning: One should pursue knowledge moment by moment and wealth grain by grain. If moments are wasted, where is knowledge? If grains are wasted, where is wealth?
slokas.when_to_use: When procrastinating or when you need a reminder that great achievements come from consistent small efforts over time.

slokas.sanskrit: अयं निजः परो वेति गणना लघुचेतसाम्। उदारचरितानां तु वसुधैव कुटुम्बकम्॥
slokas.transliteration: ayaṁ nijaḥ paro veti gaṇanā laghu-cetasām | udāra-caritānāṁ tu vasudhaiva kuṭumbakam ||
slokas.english_meaning: This one is mine, that one is a stranger — such thinking belongs to the narrow-minded. For those of noble character, the entire world is one family.
slokas.when_to_use: When encountering prejudice, discrimination, or narrow tribalism — a reminder to see all of humanity as one family.

slokas.sanskrit: परोक्षे कार्यहन्तारं प्रत्यक्षे प्रियवादिनम्। वर्जयेत्तादृशं मित्रं विषकुम्भं पयोमुखम्॥
slokas.transliteration: parokṣe kārya-hantāraṁ pratyakṣe priya-vādinam | varjayet tādṛśaṁ mitraṁ viṣa-kumbhaṁ payo-mukham ||
slokas.english_meaning: One who harms your work behind your back but speaks sweetly to your face — avoid such a friend as you would a pot of poison covered with milk on top.
slokas.when_to_use: When dealing with two-faced people who appear friendly but sabotage you behind the scenes.

slokas.sanskrit: उत्साहो बलवानार्य नास्त्युत्साहात्परं बलम्। सोत्साहस्य च लोकेषु न किञ्चिदपि दुर्लभम्॥
slokas.transliteration: utsāho balavān ārya nāsty utsāhāt paraṁ balam | sotsāhasya ca lokeṣu na kiñcid api durlabham ||
slokas.english_meaning: Enthusiasm is mighty, O noble one. There is no strength greater than enthusiasm. For one who possesses enthusiasm, nothing in this world is unattainable.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling demotivated or lacking energy to pursue your goals — a reminder that enthusiasm itself is the greatest power.

slokas.sanskrit: वाणी रसवती यस्य यस्य श्रमवती क्रिया। लक्ष्मीर्दानवती यस्य सफलं तस्य जीवितम्॥
slokas.transliteration: vāṇī rasavatī yasya yasya śramavatī kriyā | lakṣmīr dānavatī yasya saphalaṁ tasya jīvitam ||
slokas.english_meaning: One whose speech is sweet, whose actions are filled with effort, and whose wealth is shared in charity — that person's life is truly fruitful.
slokas.when_to_use: When reflecting on what makes a life truly successful — sweet speech, hard work, and generosity together.

slokas.sanskrit: आलस्यं हि मनुष्याणां शरीरस्थो महान् रिपुः। नास्त्युद्यमसमो बन्धुः कृत्वा यं नावसीदति॥
slokas.transliteration: ālasyaṁ hi manuṣyāṇāṁ śarīra-stho mahān ripuḥ | nāsty udyama-samo bandhuḥ kṛtvā yaṁ nāvasīdati ||
slokas.english_meaning: Laziness is the greatest enemy dwelling within the human body. There is no friend equal to hard work — one who embraces it never comes to grief.
slokas.when_to_use: When struggling with procrastination or laziness and needing a push to get moving.

slokas.sanskrit: गुणा गुणज्ञेषु गुणा भवन्ति ते निर्गुणं प्राप्य भवन्ति दोषाः। सुस्वादुतोयाः प्रभवन्ति नद्यः समुद्रमासाद्य भवन्त्यपेयाः॥
slokas.transliteration: guṇā guṇajñeṣu guṇā bhavanti te nirguṇaṁ prāpya bhavanti doṣāḥ | su-svādu-toyāḥ prabhavanti nadyaḥ samudram āsādya bhavanty apeyāḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Virtues are recognized as virtues only by those who appreciate them; among the unworthy, virtues become faults. Rivers flow with sweet water, but upon reaching the ocean, they become undrinkable.
slokas.when_to_use: When your efforts go unappreciated — a reminder to present your talents before those who can recognize their worth.

slokas.sanskrit: मूर्खस्य पञ्च चिह्नानि गर्वो दुर्वचनं तथा। क्रोधश्च दृढवादश्च परवाक्येष्वनादरः॥
slokas.transliteration: mūrkhasya pañca cihnāni garvo durvacanaṁ tathā | krodhaś ca dṛḍha-vādaś ca para-vākyeṣv anādaraḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: A fool has five marks: arrogance, harsh speech, anger, stubbornness in argument, and disrespect for the words of others.
slokas.when_to_use: When you notice these traits in yourself or others — a checklist for identifying and avoiding foolish behavior.

slokas.sanskrit: परोपकाराय फलन्ति वृक्षाः परोपकाराय वहन्ति नद्यः। परोपकाराय दुहन्ति गावः परोपकारार्थमिदं शरीरम्॥
slokas.transliteration: paropakārāya phalanti vṛkṣāḥ paropakārāya vahanti nadyaḥ | paropakārāya duhanti gāvaḥ paropakārārtham idaṁ śarīram ||
slokas.english_meaning: Trees bear fruit for others, rivers flow for others, cows give milk for others — this body too is meant for serving others.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling purposeless or self-centered — a reminder that the highest purpose of life is to serve others.

slokas.sanskrit: नम्रन्ति फलिनो वृक्षा नम्रन्ति गुणिनो जनाः। शुष्ककाष्ठानि मूर्खाश्च भिद्यन्ते न नमन्ति च॥
slokas.transliteration: namranti phalino vṛkṣā namranti guṇino janāḥ | śuṣka-kāṣṭhāni mūrkhāś ca bhidyante na namanti ca ||
slokas.english_meaning: Trees laden with fruit bend down; people endowed with virtue bow humbly. Dry sticks and fools only break — they never bend.
slokas.when_to_use: When success tempts you toward arrogance — a reminder that true greatness shows itself through humility.

slokas.sanskrit: धृतिः क्षमा दमोऽस्तेयं शौचमिन्द्रियनिग्रहः। धीर्विद्या सत्यमक्रोधो दशकं धर्मलक्षणम्॥
slokas.transliteration: dhṛtiḥ kṣamā damo 'steyaṁ śaucam indriya-nigrahaḥ | dhīr vidyā satyam akrodho daśakaṁ dharma-lakṣaṇam ||
slokas.english_meaning: Steadfastness, forgiveness, self-control, non-stealing, purity, sense-restraint, wisdom, knowledge, truthfulness, and freedom from anger — these ten are the marks of dharma.
slokas.when_to_use: When you need a clear framework for ethical living — ten qualities that define a person of dharma.

slokas.sanskrit: अष्टादशपुराणेषु व्यासस्य वचनद्वयम्। परोपकारः पुण्याय पापाय परपीडनम्॥
slokas.transliteration: aṣṭādaśa-purāṇeṣu vyāsasya vacana-dvayam | paropakāraḥ puṇyāya pāpāya para-pīḍanam ||
slokas.english_meaning: In all eighteen Puranas, Vyasa has said only two things: helping others is virtue, and harming others is sin.
slokas.when_to_use: When overthinking right and wrong — this distills all ethics into one simple principle: help, don't harm.

slokas.sanskrit: गुणैरुत्तमतां याति नोच्चैरासनसंस्थितः। प्रासादशिखरस्थोऽपि काकः किं गरुडायते॥
slokas.transliteration: guṇair uttamatāṁ yāti noccair āsana-saṁsthitaḥ | prāsāda-śikhara-stho 'pi kākaḥ kiṁ garuḍāyate ||
slokas.english_meaning: One attains greatness through virtues, not by sitting on a high seat. Does a crow become an eagle just by perching atop a palace?
slokas.when_to_use: When someone flaunts their position rather than their character, or when you are tempted to value titles over substance.

slokas.sanskrit: संघे शक्तिः कलौ युगे॥ एकेनापि सुवृक्षेण पुष्पितेन सुगन्धिना। वासितं तद्वनं सर्वं सुपुत्रेण कुलं यथा॥
slokas.transliteration: saṅghe śaktiḥ kalau yuge || ekenāpi su-vṛkṣeṇa puṣpitena sugandhinā | vāsitaṁ tad vanaṁ sarvaṁ su-putreṇa kulaṁ yathā ||
slokas.english_meaning: In unity lies strength. Just as a single flowering fragrant tree perfumes an entire forest, so does one worthy person bring honor to an entire family.
slokas.when_to_use: When working in teams and needing to emphasize cooperation, or when one person's excellence can uplift an entire group.

slokas.sanskrit: कृतं न शोचेदगतं न चिन्तयेत्। वर्तमानेन कालेन वर्तयन्ति विचक्षणाः॥
slokas.transliteration: kṛtaṁ na śoced agataṁ na cintayet | vartamānena kālena vartayanti vicakṣaṇāḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Do not grieve over what is past; do not worry about what has not yet come. The wise live in the present moment.
slokas.when_to_use: When caught between regret about the past and anxiety about the future — a call to focus on the present.

slokas.sanskrit: अक्रोधेन जयेत्क्रोधमसाधुं साधुना जयेत्। जयेत्कदर्यं दानेन जयेत्सत्येन चानृतम्॥
slokas.transliteration: akrodhena jayet krodham asādhuṁ sādhunā jayet | jayet kadaryaṁ dānena jayet satyena cānṛtam ||
slokas.english_meaning: Conquer anger with calmness, conquer evil with goodness, conquer the miser with generosity, and conquer falsehood with truth.
slokas.when_to_use: When provoked by anger, dishonesty, or hostility — a strategy of overcoming negativity with its opposite virtue.

slokas.sanskrit: भोगा न भुक्ता वयमेव भुक्ताः तपो न तप्तं वयमेव तप्ताः। कालो न यातो वयमेव याताः तृष्णा न जीर्णा वयमेव जीर्णाः॥
slokas.transliteration: bhogā na bhuktā vayam eva bhuktāḥ tapo na taptaṁ vayam eva taptāḥ | kālo na yāto vayam eva yātāḥ tṛṣṇā na jīrṇā vayam eva jīrṇāḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Pleasures were not enjoyed — rather, we were consumed by them. Austerity was not practiced — rather, we were scorched. Time did not pass — rather, we passed away. Desire did not grow old — rather, we grew old.
slokas.when_to_use: When wasting time chasing pleasures or when you need a sobering reminder that time is passing and desires are never fully satisfied.

slokas.sanskrit: उत्तमे न हि कर्तव्यं प्राणैः कण्ठगतैरपि। मित्राणां विपदो याति कृष्णसर्पमुखे यथा॥
slokas.transliteration: prāṇā yathātmano 'bhīṣṭā bhūtānām api te tathā | ātmaupamyena bhūtānāṁ dayāṁ kurvanti sādhavaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Just as one's own life is dear to oneself, so it is to all beings. The good show compassion to all creatures, seeing them as equal to themselves.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling indifferent to others' suffering — a reminder that every being values its life just as you do yours.

slokas.sanskrit: उद्यमेन हि सिध्यन्ति कार्याणि न मनोरथैः। न हि सुप्तस्य सिंहस्य प्रविशन्ति मुखे मृगाः॥
slokas.transliteration: udyamena hi sidhyanti kāryāṇi na manorathaiḥ | na hi suptasya siṁhasya praviśanti mukhe mṛgāḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Tasks are accomplished through effort, not by mere wishful thinking. Prey does not walk into the mouth of a sleeping lion.
slokas.when_to_use: When daydreaming instead of working, or when someone expects results without putting in the effort.

slokas.sanskrit: वज्रादपि कठोराणि मृदूनि कुसुमादपि। लोकोत्तराणां चेतांसि को नु विज्ञातुमर्हति॥
slokas.transliteration: vajrād api kaṭhorāṇi mṛdūni kusumād api | lokottarāṇāṁ cetāṁsi ko nu vijñātum arhati ||
slokas.english_meaning: Harder than a thunderbolt, yet softer than a flower — who can truly understand the hearts of extraordinary beings?
slokas.when_to_use: When trying to understand people of great depth who can be both strong and gentle — a reminder that true character is multifaceted.

slokas.sanskrit: क्षमा बलमशक्तानां शक्तानां भूषणं क्षमा। क्षमा वशीकृते लोके क्षमया किं न सिध्यति॥
slokas.transliteration: kṣamā balam aśaktānāṁ śaktānāṁ bhūṣaṇaṁ kṣamā | kṣamā vaśīkṛte loke kṣamayā kiṁ na sidhyati ||
slokas.english_meaning: Patience is the strength of the weak and the ornament of the strong. Patience conquers the world — what cannot be achieved through patience?
slokas.when_to_use: When impatience is getting the better of you, or when you need to remind yourself that patience is not weakness but strength.

slokas.sanskrit: दानेन तुल्यो विधिरस्ति नान्यो लोभेन तुल्यो विधिरस्ति नान्यः। लोभं विहाय विमलं चरित्रं कुर्यात्सदा धर्मपथे प्रवृत्तिम्॥
slokas.transliteration: kṛpणena samо dātuṁ sadyo yaḥ prabhavaty alam | tasya vittaṁ haranty ete rājakālabalīyasaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: There is no merit equal to generosity and no vice equal to greed. Abandon greed and maintain a pure character, always walking the path of dharma.
slokas.when_to_use: When tempted by greed or when you need motivation to be more generous with your time, money, or knowledge.

slokas.sanskrit: सर्वनाशे समुत्पन्ने अर्धं त्यजति पण्डितः। अर्धेन कुरुते कार्यं सर्वनाशो हि दुःसहः॥
slokas.transliteration: sarva-nāśe samutpanne ardhaṁ tyajati paṇḍitaḥ | ardhena kurute kāryaṁ sarva-nāśo hi duḥsahaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: When total destruction looms, the wise person sacrifices half to save the rest. With the remaining half, work can continue — total destruction is unbearable.
slokas.when_to_use: When facing a situation where you must cut losses — a reminder that strategic sacrifice is wiser than losing everything.

slokas.sanskrit: येषां न विद्या न तपो न दानं ज्ञानं न शीलं न गुणो न धर्मः। ते मर्त्यलोके भुवि भारभूता मनुष्यरूपेण मृगाश्चरन्ति॥
slokas.transliteration: yeṣāṁ na vidyā na tapo na dānaṁ jñānaṁ na śīlaṁ na guṇo na dharmaḥ | te martya-loke bhuvi bhāra-bhūtā manuṣya-rūpeṇa mṛgāś caranti ||
slokas.english_meaning: Those who have neither learning, nor austerity, nor charity, nor wisdom, nor character, nor virtue, nor righteousness — they are merely animals roaming the earth in human form, a burden upon the world.
slokas.when_to_use: When you need a strong wake-up call to develop yourself and not waste your human potential.

slokas.sanskrit: कार्यार्थी भजते लोकं यावत्कार्यं न सिध्यति। उत्तीर्णे च परे पारे नौकायां किं प्रयोजनम्॥
slokas.transliteration: kāryārthī bhajate lokaṁ yāvat kāryaṁ na sidhyati | uttīrṇe ca pare pāre naukāyāṁ kiṁ prayojanam ||
slokas.english_meaning: A selfish person courts others only until their purpose is served. Once they have crossed to the other shore, what use is the boat?
slokas.when_to_use: When someone uses you only for their benefit — a warning to recognize transactional relationships.

slokas.sanskrit: गच्छन् पिपीलको याति योजनानां शतान्यपि। अगच्छन् वैनतेयोऽपि पदमेकं न गच्छति॥
slokas.transliteration: gacchan pipīliko yāti yojanānāṁ śatāny api | agacchan vainateyo 'pi padam ekaṁ na gacchati ||
slokas.english_meaning: An ant, by continuing to move, covers hundreds of miles. But even Garuda (the mighty eagle), if it does not move, cannot take a single step.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling that your efforts are too small to matter — a reminder that persistent small steps outperform idle greatness.
